# unifi_setting_guest_access (Resource)

Manages UniFi guest access and captive portal settings. This is a singleton resource — one per site. Destroying resets to defaults.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "unifi_setting_guest_access" "example" {
  portal_enabled          = true
  portal_customized       = true
  auth                    = "none"
  portal_customized_title = "Welcome to Our Network"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `auth` (String) Authentication type. Valid values: 'none', 'password', 'hotspot', 'radius', 'custom'.
- `ec_enabled` (Boolean) Enable external captive portal.
- `expire` (Number) Guest expiration in minutes.
- `expire_number` (Number) Number of expire units.
- `expire_unit` (Number) Expire unit (1=hours, 60=minutes, 1440=days).
- `portal_customized` (Boolean) Enable portal customization.
- `portal_customized_authentication_text` (String) Portal authentication prompt text.
- `portal_customized_bg_color` (String) Portal background color (hex).
- `portal_customized_bg_image_enabled` (Boolean) Enable portal background image.
- `portal_customized_bg_type` (String) Portal background type.
- `portal_customized_box_color` (String) Portal box color (hex).
- `portal_customized_box_opacity` (Number) Portal box opacity (0-100).
- `portal_customized_box_radius` (Number) Portal box border radius.
- `portal_customized_box_text_color` (String) Portal box text color (hex).
- `portal_customized_button_color` (String) Portal button color (hex).
- `portal_customized_button_text` (String) Portal button text.
- `portal_customized_button_text_color` (String) Portal button text color (hex).
- `portal_customized_link_color` (String) Portal link color (hex).
- `portal_customized_logo_position` (String) Portal logo position.
- `portal_customized_logo_size` (Number) Portal logo size.
- `portal_customized_success_text` (String) Portal success text.
- `portal_customized_text_color` (String) Portal text color (hex).
- `portal_customized_title` (String) Portal page title.
- `portal_customized_welcome_text` (String) Portal welcome text.
- `portal_enabled` (Boolean) Enable the guest portal.
- `portal_use_hostname` (Boolean) Use hostname for portal.
- `redirect_enabled` (Boolean) Enable redirect after authentication.
- `redirect_https` (Boolean) Enable HTTPS redirect.
- `redirect_to_https` (Boolean) Redirect to HTTPS.
- `restricted_subnet_1` (String) First restricted subnet (CIDR).
- `restricted_subnet_2` (String) Second restricted subnet (CIDR).
- `restricted_subnet_3` (String) Third restricted subnet (CIDR).
- `template_engine` (String) Template engine. Valid values: 'angular', 'jsp'.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `site_id` (String)

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Guest access settings can be imported using the setting ID:

```shell
terraform import unifi_setting_guest_access.example 60a1b2c3d4e5f67890123456
```
